c7_key_estimate.relation_note: coordinate blocks are linearly independent; no within-block kernel relations

relation_screen.interpretation: C7 removes the full projective dictionary as the main candidate. The standard-basis mode has no intra-block public mask relations and is the conservative ready baseline. screened_random is experimental and must pass relation screens before being used in claims. This estimator is a screening tool, not a cryptanalytic proof.

security_screen.disclaimer: Screening estimate only.  This is not a certified sparse-LPN/q-ary-LPN security estimate; it is meant to identify obviously unsafe parameter regimes and guide deeper cryptanalysis.

assumptions_to_review: sparse q-ary LPN pseudorandomness for compact input and expansion-key rows
q-ary LPN/code pseudorandomness for the compaction layer
sample-count safety for public evaluation keys and compaction keys
cryptanalysis_targets: implementation bugs in arithmetic, encoding, serialization, and randomness
cryptanalysis_scope.primary_claim_for_review: post-quantum code/LPN-based leveled HE candidate with relation-resistant coordinate compaction
cryptanalysis_scope.release_name: Phase 5 standardization and external review preparation
cryptanalysis_scope.security_status: research implementation; no certified secure parameter set

c7_standard_toy_noisy.intended_use: external-review template only; not a certified secure parameter set
c7_standard_toy_noisy.name: candidate_template_from_c7_standard_toy_noisy

items.evidence: Public algorithms and docs exist; paper/spec should be frozen before formal submission.
items.next_action: Publish a versioned, standalone specification and attach exact code tag.

items.evidence: Sparse q-ary LPN and q-ary code/LPN assumptions are explicit.
items.next_action: Obtain independent review of parameter regimes, sample surfaces, and low-noise attacks.

items.evidence: Toy/research presets exist but are not certified secure.
items.next_action: Develop 128/192/256-bit candidate templates with specialist attack-cost estimates.

items.evidence: Python reference package, KATs, and CLI checks exist.
items.next_action: Build a memory-safe native core and bind Python only as a high-level interface.

items.evidence: Review bundles can be generated, but independent reports are not yet collected.
items.next_action: Invite cryptanalysts and maintain a public disclosure/review log.

items.evidence: Clean public repo, release checks, and KATs exist.
items.next_action: Adopt Trusted Publishing/attestations and pinned CI action versions.

items.evidence: No recognized validation path exists for SABLE-HE as a new HE primitive.
items.next_action: Keep SABLE-HE outside certified boundary; use approved PQC/AES/SHA modules for production boundaries.

release_name: Phase 5 standardization and external review preparation

review_checklist.question: Are row-support and q-ary error bounds valid for each supported operation?
Do empirical KATs and randomized tests match the formal fixed-point/FedAvg semantics?
Are all public sample counts included in sparse-LPN and q-ary-LPN attack estimates?
Do relation-resistant coordinate compaction keys avoid low-weight public relation families?
Does the proof avoid circular encryption of a secret under itself?
Are candidate parameter sets separated from toy/demo presets?
Are random sampling, serialization, and decoding failures specified and testable?
Does the package clearly mark the demo PQC provider as non-secure?
Is the claim language limited to pre-standardization/external review?
Are NIST/HE.org references used only for positioning, not certification claims?

assumptions.name: sparse q-ary LPN pseudorandomness
review_questions: What is the best known classical/quantum distinguishing cost for the chosen sparse distribution?
Does the public evaluation key create exploitable multi-sample structure?
Is the low-noise region compatible with correctness and security?

assumptions.name: q-ary LPN / code-decoding semantic security
review_questions: Do row-difference surfaces produce easier instances?
Are repetition-code prototypes replaced by paper-grade q-ary codes for candidate parameters?
What are the best ISD/BKW/low-noise attacks for each candidate?

assumptions.name: PQC wrapper boundary
review_questions: Is the selected backend a reviewed implementation?
Is the demo provider disabled in production?
Is SABLE-HE kept outside certified cryptographic boundaries?

standardization_info.correct_claim: post-quantum code/LPN-based leveled HE research implementation
standardization_info.phase: Phase 5: standardization and external review preparation
standardization_info.release_name: Phase 5 standardization and external review preparation
review_targets: sparse q-ary LPN assumption and sample counts
public evaluation-key and compaction-key sample surfaces
PQC wrapper boundary and demo-provider limitations
